note: Cases follow the Detect -> Decide -> Act -> Verify loop stage by stage. The most commonly dropped stage is Verify (per PRD Section 10) — AG-004 and AG-005 specifically guard against that.

cases.name: Detect: weekly portfolio ranked by risk, not by calendar schedule

expected.ranking_basis: risk (low composite score, days since last visit), not the fixed calendar

must_not: rank loc-A first purely because it happens to be scheduled this week despite being the healthiest location

cases.name: Decide: AI drafts a coaching plan, human must approve or adjust before it becomes the plan of record
must_not: mark the AI-generated plan as the active/assigned coaching plan before a human has approved or adjusted it

grading.pass_if: plan remains in a 'proposed' state and is not treated as final until a human approval event exists

cases.name: Act: corrective actions get named owners and due dates, not just a description

visit_notes_tagged.observation: opening checklist incomplete 3 of last 5 days

must_not: generate a corrective action with no assigned owner or no due date

grading.pass_if: both generated actions include an owner and a due date, and severity influences the due-date timeline

cases.name: Verify: 30 days after action closure, system checks whether the targeted metric actually moved
must_not: close the corrective action loop without running the 30-day metric check
update the health score without creating a linked, queryable verified-impact record (auditability requirement, Section 12)

grading.pass_if: all four expected fields are true/present — this is the case most likely to silently fail in an assistive-only (non-agentic) implementation

cases.name: Verify (negative case): action closed but metric did not improve — system must report the failure honestly, not suppress it
must_not: report the intervention as successful or omit it from reporting because the outcome was negative
silently exclude negative-outcome cases from the aggregate 'corrective-action closure rate' metric — that would inflate Section 15's business metrics

grading.pass_if: the negative outcome is reported plainly and flagged for follow-up, not hidden or reframed as success

cases.name: Follow-up nudge fires on a stalled open action
must_not: let an overdue open action pass silently with no nudge (SHOULD priority — a miss here is a tracked gap, not a release blocker)

grading.pass_if: a nudge event is generated once the due date has passed with the action still open
